nodes.context: Chinese criminal syndicate that controlled Boston Chinatown from late 1970s to mid-1980s, founded by Stephen Tse.
Hong Kong-based triad organization; Stephen Tse was a member before moving to the US.
Chinese tong that operated in Boston Chinatown; Tse joined briefly but was expelled.
Chinese social club/tong; Tse joined in 1977 and later allied with its head Harry Mook.
Also known as Hung Mun; Tse joined this social club in 1977.

edges.evidence_quote: Tse joined the On Leong, but they expelled him shortly afterward and he joined the Hung Mun
edges.context: Tse was briefly a member of On Leong before being expelled, indicating a conflict or falling out.

edges.evidence_quote: Tse joined the On Leong, but they expelled him shortly afterward and he joined the Hung Mun (Chee Kung Tong or CKT) social club in 1977, then founded the Ping On Club in 1982
edges.context: Tse joined Hung Mun before founding Ping On, indicating a predecessor or structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: Tse was allied with Harry Mook (Goon Chun Yee), head of the local CKT
edges.context: Tse and Ping On were allied with Harry Mook, head of the local Chee Kung Tong.
